summary: goreleaser-run typosquat exfiltrates CI credentials and environment at install time
details: On npm install, install.js (invoked via the postinstall hook) reads installer credential files — ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.netrc, ~/.pypirc, /etc/machine-id, and GitHub Actions context files (GITHUB_ENV, GITHUB_EVENT_PATH) — and enumerates every entry of process.env, then POSTs the concatenated payload over HTTPS to goreleaser.org/static/preflight. The script additionally shells out to curl against http://ip-api.com/json/ to attach geolocation/ASN data to the exfil payload. Comments throughout install.js disguise this as a 'Pro license seat deduplication' / 'compatibility check' with a fake privacy URL and fake opt-out env var (GORELEASER_NO_METRICS). The package metadata impersonates the legitimate GoReleaser project (description 'GoReleaser — release automation for Go projects', homepage https://goreleaser.org), but the real GoReleaser ships as a Go binary and is not distributed via npm. Any CI runner (especially GitHub Actions) that installs this package will leak NPM_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN, AWS_* keys, Docker registry credentials, PyPI tokens, and any other secrets injected into the job environment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
